questionPrompt: Some people think that employees should work from home, while others believe working in an office is more effective. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essayText:
Nowadays many people have opportunity to work from home because technology develop very fast. Some people think it is good because people can save time and money, but others think office is better place for work. In my opinion, working from home have advantages but also disadvantages.

Firstly, working from home is comfortable. People do not need wake up early and travel to office. This help them reduce stress and save money for transport. For example, workers can start work faster and have more time for family. Also, home environment can be quiet and good for concentration.

However, working in office allow better communication. Workers can talk directly with colleagues and manager. This can improve teamwork and reduce misunderstanding. Also, some people feel lonely when they work alone for long time.

In conclusion, both methods have benefits. I think working from home is useful sometimes but office work is still important for cooperation.

questionPrompt: Some people think governments should spend more money on sports facilities. Others think money should be spent on healthcare. Discuss both views.
essayText:
Many countries have problem with health of people. Some people think government should spend money on sport facilities because sport help people stay healthy. Other people think healthcare is more important because hospitals save lives.

Sport facilities can prevent illness. People who do exercise often have strong body and less stress. If government build gyms or parks more people can be active. This reduce obesity and heart disease.

However, hospitals and doctors are necessary because not all illness can be prevented. People sometimes need medicine or surgery. Healthcare help people live longer and reduce pain.

In conclusion, both are important but I think healthcare should receive more money because treatment is necessary for serious diseases.

questionPrompt: Some people believe online learning is better than traditional classroom learning. Discuss both views.
essayText:
Online education become more popular in recent years. Some people think online learning is better because students can study anytime and anywhere. Others believe traditional classroom learning is more effective because of direct communication.

Online learning gives flexibility. Students can watch lectures many times and choose their own schedule. This is useful for people who work or live far from universities. Also, online courses often cost less money.

However, classroom learning has advantages too. Students can ask questions immediately and teachers can explain difficult topics more clearly. Communication with classmates can also improve motivation and understanding.

In conclusion, online learning is convenient but traditional education is still important because interaction helps students learn better.

taskResponse.reason: Ideas relevant but somewhat general.

questionPrompt: Some people think young people should travel abroad before starting work. Discuss advantages and disadvantages.
essayText:
Travelling abroad before starting career become popular idea. Some people think it is useful because young people learn new culture and language. Others think it delay career development.

One advantage is gaining experience. When young people travel they meet new people and understand different traditions. This can increase independence and confidence. Also, language skills may improve.

However, travelling can be expensive and take time. Some people may lose job opportunities because they start work later. In addition, not all travel experiences give useful knowledge for career.

In conclusion, travelling abroad have both benefits and drawbacks. It is good for personal development but young people should consider their financial situation.

Environment problem become serious in modern world. Some people think individuals should protect environment by recycling and saving energy. Others think government should make laws to reduce pollution.

Individuals can help environment by simple actions. For example, people can use less plastic and save electricity. If many people change habits environment can improve.

However, government have more power. They can introduce laws and control factories. Large companies produce big pollution so government regulation is important.

In conclusion, both individuals and government should work together to protect environment.

questionPrompt: Some people believe that students should be allowed to evaluate their teachers, while others think this is not a good idea. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essayText:
In many education systems, the role of students is usually limited to attending lessons and completing assignments. However, some people argue that students should also be allowed to evaluate their teachers. While this idea may help improve teaching quality, others believe students are not experienced enough to make fair judgments. In my opinion, students should be able to give feedback, but this should be done in a structured and balanced way.

On the one hand, allowing students to evaluate teachers can provide useful information for schools and institutions. Students spend many hours with teachers and directly experience their teaching methods, classroom management, and communication style. Because of this, they can often identify whether lessons are engaging, clear, and helpful. For example, if many students report that a teacher explains concepts poorly or does not respond to questions, the school may recognise a problem that needs attention. In this way, student evaluations can support professional improvement.

On the other hand, there are valid concerns about fairness and accuracy. Students may judge teachers based on personal preferences rather than actual teaching quality. For instance, a strict teacher who gives challenging homework may receive lower evaluations even if their teaching is effective. In addition, younger students may not have enough maturity or academic understanding to assess teaching standards properly. As a result, student opinions alone should not be the only factor in evaluating teachers.

In conclusion, student evaluation of teachers can be beneficial because it gives schools insight into classroom experience. However, it should be combined with other measures such as peer reviews, academic results, and observations by school leaders. This balanced approach would make the evaluation process more fair and useful.

taskResponse.reason: Both views are discussed clearly and the writer gives a direct opinion. Ideas are relevant and sufficiently developed, though some points could be expanded further.

questionPrompt: Some people think that modern technology is making people more socially isolated, while others believe it is helping people stay connected. Discuss both views and give your opinion.
essayText:
Modern technology has changed the way people communicate in everyday life. Some people argue that it causes social isolation because individuals spend too much time on phones and computers, while others believe it helps people stay connected regardless of distance. In my opinion, technology can strengthen relationships, but only when it is used in a balanced way.

There are several reasons why people think technology increases isolation. First, many individuals now prefer online interaction instead of face-to-face communication. It is common to see family members sitting together while paying more attention to their screens than to each other. This can weaken real social bonds and reduce the quality of personal interaction. Second, social media sometimes creates only superficial contact. People may have hundreds of online friends, but this does not always mean they have deep or meaningful relationships.

However, technology also brings major social benefits. Messaging applications, video calls, and social platforms allow people to maintain contact with relatives and friends who live far away. In the past, distance was a serious barrier to communication, but today people can speak instantly across countries. This is particularly useful for international students, migrant workers, and families living in different regions. Technology also allows people with similar interests to build communities online, which can reduce loneliness rather than increase it.

In conclusion, modern technology has both positive and negative effects on social life. It may contribute to isolation when it replaces genuine human interaction, but it also offers powerful tools that help people communicate more easily. Overall, I believe the impact depends more on how people use technology than on the technology itself.

taskResponse.reason: The response addresses all parts of the task and provides a clear opinion. Main ideas are relevant, though development is somewhat predictable.

teacherReview.teacherNotes: A solid mid-band essay. Clear, organised, and relevant, but vocabulary and grammar do not show enough flexibility for Band 7+.

questionPrompt: Some people believe that governments should spend more money on public transport, while others think building more roads is a better solution to traffic problems. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
essayText:
Traffic congestion is a major issue in many cities around the world. Some people think that the best way to solve this problem is to invest in public transport, while others believe that constructing more roads is more effective. I believe that improving public transport is a better long-term solution, although road development may still be necessary in some cases.

Those who support building more roads argue that it can reduce pressure on existing transport infrastructure. In cities where the number of vehicles has increased rapidly, roads often become overcrowded and inefficient. Creating wider roads, additional lanes, or new highways may improve traffic flow in the short term. This can be especially useful in developing urban areas where infrastructure has not kept up with population growth.

However, expanding road networks does not always solve congestion permanently. In many cases, better roads encourage more people to use private cars, which eventually leads to the same problem again. By contrast, investment in public transport can reduce the number of cars on the road by providing a cheaper, faster, and more environmentally friendly alternative. Reliable buses, trains, and metro systems make it possible for large numbers of people to travel efficiently. This not only addresses congestion but also reduces pollution and fuel consumption.

In conclusion, although road construction may provide temporary relief, governments should prioritise public transport because it offers broader and more sustainable benefits. In my view, a transport policy focused mainly on efficient public systems is the most effective way to solve traffic problems in growing cities.

taskResponse.reason: The response fully addresses both views and provides a clear, well-supported opinion. Ideas are relevant and sufficiently extended.

In conclusion, homework is useful but should not be excessive.
